extra.message: Server-Side Template Injection via Jinja2. The user-controlled 'name' query parameter (line 19) is interpolated into an f-string on line 38 to construct 'content', which is then passed to Template(content).render() on line 41. An attacker can inject Jinja2 template syntax such as {{7*7}} or more dangerous expressions like {{config.__class__.__init__.__globals__['os'].popen('id').read()}} to achieve Remote Code Execution.

cwe: CWE-1336: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in a Templating Engine
CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code
CWE-95: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code

extra.message: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF). The PUBLIC_IP_SERVICE_URL is loaded from an environment variable (config.py line 11) with no URL validation, allowlist, or sanitization. The requests.get() call on line 31 uses this unvalidated URL directly. An attacker who can control the environment variable (via container orchestration misconfiguration, .env file manipulation, or other means) can force the server to make HTTP requests to arbitrary internal or external URLs, potentially accessing internal services, metadata endpoints, or other sensitive resources.

Hardcoded credentials exposed in source code. SUPER_SECRET_NAME and SUPER_SECRET_TOKEN are hardcoded string literals (lines 13-15) instead of being loaded from environment variables. The FIXME comments indicate these should use os.getenv(). Hardcoded secrets in version control can be compromised if the repository is exposed, and are particularly dangerous for authentication tokens.
